title: Medical secretaries
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate medical and health services in hospitals, clinics, managed care organizations, public health agencies, or similar organizations. Transcribe medical reports recorded by physicians and other healthcare practitioners using various electronic devices, covering office visits, emergency room visits, diagnostic imaging studies, operations, chart reviews, and final summaries. Transcribe dictated reports and translate abbreviations into fully understandable form. Edit as necessary and return reports in either printed or electronic form for review and signature, or correction. Perform secretarial duties using specific knowledge of medical terminology and hospital, clinic, or laboratory procedures. Duties may include scheduling appointments, billing patients, and compiling and recording medical charts, reports, and correspondence.

tasks.task: Maintain medical records, correspondence files, and prepare and compile medical charts, reports, or correspondence using typewriter or personal computer.

Translate medical jargon and abbreviations into their expanded forms to ensure the accuracy of patient and healthcare facility records and distinguish between homonyms, recognizing inconsistencies and mistakes in medical terms.

Perform various clerical or administrative functions, including ordering and maintaining an inventory of supplies, handling incoming and outgoing mail, and scheduling tests or procedures based on physician orders.

Transcribe recorded messages, practitioners' diagnoses or recommendations, and dictation for various medical reports such as patient histories, physical examinations, and discharge summaries, reviewing and editing for spelling, grammar, and clarity.
Complete insurance or other claim forms and prepare financial records, including bookkeeping duties such as credits or collections and preparing and sending financial statements or bills.

Operate office equipment and use software applications to prepare reports, invoices, financial statements, letters, case histories, or medical records, ensuring efficient data entry and retrieval services.
Receive patients, schedule appointments, and maintain patient records, confirming diagnostic appointments, surgeries, or medical consultations as needed.
Greet visitors, ascertain the purpose of their visit, receive and screen telephone calls, and direct them to appropriate staff.
Transmit correspondence or medical records by mail, e-mail, or fax, and return dictated reports in printed or electronic form for physician's review.
Interview patients to complete documents such as intake or insurance forms and check for mistakes in reports, obtaining correct information from doctors when necessary.
Maintain awareness of advances in medicine, health insurance changes, and financing options to support administrative functions within the facility.
Develop and maintain computerized record management systems to store data and produce reports, and organize the facility's policies and procedures.
Develop instructional materials and conduct in-service and community-based educational programs, in addition to overseeing the training and performance evaluation of medical and clerical staff.
Consult with medical, business, and community groups to improve public relations and coordinate activities that enhance health programs and respond to community needs.
Inspect facilities and recommend modifications to ensure readiness and compliance with safety and sanitation regulations.
Answer inquiries concerning the progress of medical cases, while adhering to confidentiality laws.

detailed_work_activities.title: Perform clerical work in medical settings.
Implement organizational process or policy changes.
Interview employees, customers, or others to collect information.
Record vital statistics or other health information.
Prepare documentation for contracts, transactions, or regulatory compliance.
Communicate detailed medical information to patients or family members.
Answer telephones to direct calls or provide information.
Monitor performance of organizational members or partners.
Inspect condition or functioning of facilities or equipment.
Analyze risks to minimize losses or damages.
Maintain knowledge of current developments in area of expertise.
Develop operating strategies, plans, or procedures.
Develop procedures to evaluate organizational activities.
Prepare operational progress or status reports.
Liaise between departments or other groups to improve function or communication.
Coordinate operational activities with external stakeholders.
Manage operations, research, or logistics projects.
Prepare staff schedules or work assignments.
Advise others on legal or regulatory compliance matters.
